joke: I asked my dog what’s two minus two. He said nothing.
I told my suitcase there’d be no vacation this year. Now I’m dealing with emotional baggage.
I used to hate facial hair, but then it grew on me.
I’m reading a book about anti-gravity. It’s impossible to put down.
I only know 25 letters of the alphabet. I don’t know y.
I used to be addicted to soap, but I’m clean now.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
I tried to catch some fog earlier. Mist.
I told my fridge we needed to break up. It was just too cold.
Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
I used to play piano by ear, but now I use my hands.
I got fired from the keyboard factory. I wasn’t putting in enough shifts.
What do you call fake spaghetti? An impasta.
I used to be afraid of mirrors, but I've been reflecting on it.
I told a joke about glue once, but it just didn’t stick.
I don’t trust stairs. They’re always up to something.
I joined an origami club, but I folded under pressure.
I got fired from the calendar factory. All I did was take a day off.
My wallet is like an onion: opening it makes me cry.
I tried to start a hide-and-seek club, but good players are hard to find.
Comedian: I stayed up all night wondering where the sun went, and then it dawned on me.
I was going to tell a joke about a window, but you’d see right through it.
I wasn’t going to get a brain transplant, but then I changed my mind.
I used to be a photographer, but I just couldn’t focus.
I bought some Velcro yesterday. What a rip-off.
Comedian: My chair and I go way back. It's always been supportive.
I used to have a job drilling holes for water, but it was well boring.
I don't trust atoms. They make up everything.
I told my belt a joke, but it just buckled under the pressure.
I broke up with my pencil. It had no point.
